cards.definition: to succeed in completing a task
cards.example: The team accomplished all of its quarterly goals ahead of schedule.

cards.definition: to provide what is needed or requested
cards.example: The hotel can accommodate up to 200 guests.

cards.definition: to gather or build up over time
cards.example: Interest will accumulate on the outstanding balance each month.

cards.definition: to buy or obtain something
cards.example: The firm plans to acquire a smaller competitor next quarter.

cards.definition: next to or near something
cards.example: The new parking lot is adjacent to the main office building.

cards.definition: in a sufficient or satisfactory manner
cards.example: Please ensure that all staff members are adequately trained.

cards.definition: reasonably priced; not too expensive
cards.example: We are looking for affordable office space downtown.

cards.definition: more than enough in quantity
cards.example: There is ample parking available for conference attendees.

cards.definition: happening once every year
cards.example: The annual shareholder meeting is scheduled for March.

cards.definition: to expect or predict something will happen
cards.example: We anticipate a significant increase in demand this summer.

cards.definition: a formal assessment of value or performance
cards.example: Each employee receives an annual performance appraisal.

cards.definition: a person learning a trade under supervision
cards.example: The apprentice is training under the lead electrician.

cards.definition: to put parts together or gather people
cards.example: Workers assemble the components on the factory floor.

cards.definition: to evaluate or estimate the nature of something
cards.example: The inspector will assess the building for safety compliance.

cards.definition: a valuable item or resource owned by a company
cards.example: The company listed all of its assets in the financial report.

cards.definition: to give someone a particular task or role
cards.example: The manager will assign each team member a specific project.

cards.definition: to succeed in reaching a goal
cards.example: She attained the highest sales record in the region.

cards.definition: a person qualified to practice law
cards.example: Our attorney reviewed the terms of the merger agreement.

cards.definition: an official examination of financial records
cards.example: The external audit confirmed that all accounts were accurate.

cards.definition: to give official permission for something
cards.example: Only the director can authorize expenses over ten thousand dollars.

cards.definition: to make a process operate without human intervention
cards.example: The company decided to automate its inventory tracking system.

cards.definition: clothing, especially for a particular occasion
cards.example: Business attire is required for the client presentation.

cards.definition: to suspend a meeting until a later time
cards.example: The chairperson decided to adjourn the meeting until Friday.

cards.definition: a period of training under a skilled worker
cards.example: He completed a two-year apprenticeship in graphic design.

cards.definition: the process of putting parts together
cards.example: The assembly of the new product line begins next week.

cards.definition: unable to pay outstanding debts
cards.example: The retailer went bankrupt after three consecutive years of losses.

cards.definition: a formal large meal for many people
cards.example: The awards banquet will be held at the Grand Hotel.

cards.definition: something bought at a lower price than usual
cards.example: Shoppers found great bargains during the clearance sale.

cards.definition: a standard used for comparison or measurement
cards.example: Customer satisfaction scores serve as a benchmark for success.

cards.definition: a drink of any type
cards.example: Complimentary beverages will be served during the reception.

cards.definition: a detailed plan or technical drawing
cards.example: The architect presented the blueprint for the new office tower.

cards.definition: a group of directors managing a company
cards.example: The board approved the proposal for international expansion.

cards.definition: a small thin book with information or instructions
cards.example: Each participant received a booklet describing the training modules.

cards.definition: to increase or improve something
cards.example: The promotional campaign helped boost sales by fifteen percent.

cards.definition: a local office of a larger organization
cards.example: The bank is opening a new branch in the suburbs.

cards.definition: a printed pamphlet with product or service information
cards.example: Please pick up a brochure at the information desk.

cards.definition: a person who arranges business deals for others
cards.example: The real estate broker negotiated a favorable lease for us.

cards.definition: for a short time or in few words
cards.example: The director briefly outlined the changes to the travel policy.

cards.definition: a large quantity of goods
cards.example: Ordering supplies in bulk reduces the overall cost.

cards.definition: a heavy load or difficult responsibility
cards.example: New regulations placed an additional burden on small businesses.

cards.definition: involving two parties or countries
cards.example: The two nations signed a bilateral trade agreement.

cards.definition: an offer to do work or supply goods at a price
cards.example: Three construction firms submitted bids for the renovation project.

cards.definition: a meeting to give information or instructions
cards.example: The morning briefing covered updates on the product launch.

cards.definition: the process of sending invoices for payment
cards.example: Please contact the billing department if you have questions.

cards.definition: the process of getting on a plane or ship
cards.example: Boarding will begin at gate twelve in thirty minutes.

cards.definition: relating to plants or plant science
cards.example: The company retreat will include a tour of the botanical garden.

cards.definition: to go around or avoid something
cards.example: The new highway will bypass the congested downtown area.

cards.definition: the point where revenue equals costs
cards.example: The startup expects to reach breakeven by the end of the year.

cards.definition: ordered but not yet available for delivery
cards.example: The item is currently backordered and will ship next month.

cards.definition: high-speed internet connection
cards.example: The office upgraded to broadband for faster data transfers.

cards.definition: in a steady and unchanging manner
cards.example: The branch has consistently exceeded its monthly sales targets.

cards.definition: an official document proving a qualification or fact
cards.example: She earned a certificate in project management last year.

cards.definition: to make something easier to understand
cards.example: Could you clarify the terms of the warranty agreement?

cards.definition: a specific section in a legal document
cards.example: The penalty clause states the consequences for late delivery.

cards.definition: the regular customers of a business
cards.example: The restaurant attracts a loyal clientele from the business district.

cards.definition: to work jointly with others on a project
cards.example: The two departments will collaborate on the marketing campaign.

cards.definition: property pledged as security for a loan
cards.example: The bank required collateral before approving the business loan.

cards.definition: to honor the memory of a person or event
cards.example: The company held a ceremony to commemorate its fiftieth anniversary.

cards.definition: a regular journey between home and work
cards.example: His daily commute takes about forty-five minutes by train.

cards.definition: to pay someone for work or loss
cards.example: The company will compensate employees for overtime hours worked.

cards.definition: having sufficient skill or knowledge
cards.example: She is a highly competent manager with ten years of experience.

cards.definition: to act in accordance with rules or requests
cards.example: All employees must comply with the safety regulations.

cards.definition: including all or nearly all aspects of something
cards.example: The report provides a comprehensive analysis of market trends.

cards.definition: intended to be kept secret
cards.example: All salary information is strictly confidential.

cards.definition: overcrowding that slows movement or progress
cards.example: Traffic congestion caused many employees to arrive late.

cards.definition: following each other without interruption
cards.example: Sales have increased for five consecutive quarters.

cards.definition: to combine several things into a single unit
cards.example: The company plans to consolidate its three warehouses into one.

cards.definition: an expert who gives professional advice
cards.example: We hired a consultant to improve our supply chain efficiency.

cards.definition: a person or firm hired to do specific work
cards.example: The contractor finished the office renovation on schedule.

cards.definition: by a notably large amount or degree
cards.example: Operating costs have decreased considerably since the restructuring.

cards.definition: a person or company that delivers packages
cards.example: The documents were sent by courier for overnight delivery.

cards.definition: qualifications or achievements that prove ability
cards.example: Her impressive credentials made her the top choice for the role.

cards.definition: to reduce or limit something
cards.example: Management decided to curtail spending on non-essential travel.

cards.definition: usual or typical in a particular situation
cards.example: It is customary to provide two weeks of notice before resigning.

cards.definition: the act of following rules or standards
cards.example: The factory passed the compliance inspection without any issues.
